data.settleTest: Procurement-scope documentation for Central European operators (hyperscaler/colo country teams) showing whether rack-PDU vendor selection happens at global HQ vs. regional level, plus the share of the '40% large-operator' cell that is actually globally-contracted and unreachable in 12 months. This is knowable today from published vendor lists, procurement portals (e.g., Achilles), and a handful of operator/distributor interviews — no time needs to pass.

proposedCorrection.rationale: The 0.40 figure conflates all large-operator PDU spend with reachable spend; the globally-contracted hyperscale/pan-EU colo portion (Equinix, Digital Realty, hyperscalers on Achilles-style global lists) is not addressable by a CE regional team in Year 1, so the accessible large-operator cell should be marked down to the regionally-sourced remainder.

researchQueries.query: Do Central European colocation providers (e.g., Greenergy Estonia, Atman Poland, GTS/Vantage regional sites) source rack PDUs at country level, and what documented cases exist of a new PDU vendor entering an approved list via a regional pilot within 12 months?
What share of Equinix and Digital Realty rack-PDU purchasing across EMEA is locked to HQ-signed multi-year global vendor contracts (Vertiv/Legrand/Schneider) with no regional deviation authority?
